summary: Keyword co-occurrence in bundled CLI requires human review
details: The bundled CLI files (dist/cli.cjs and dist/cli.js) contain keyword co-occurrence of 'ping' and 'POST' that pattern-matched a network-command-exfiltration rule. This is keyword proximity in a minified/bundled output, not a traced exfiltration path — the same shape commonly arises from legitimate health-check / telemetry / network-utility code being bundled alongside HTTP request handling. Without semantic verification of the actual code paths around these literals, intent cannot be determined. Routing to human review so a reviewer can inspect dist/cli.cjs around line 3260 and dist/cli.js around line 3253 to confirm whether the 'ping' invocations and 'POST' calls form an exfiltration path or are independent, benign features of the CLI.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
